7A.content: Hey! Saw you joined the Signet Discord - welcome! Have you had a chance to poke around yet? Happy to point you in the right direction if helpful. -Jake

7A.subject: Quick question about your Signet setup
7A.content:
Hey!

Noticed you joined our Discord but haven't installed Signet yet. Totally fine - just wanted to check in.

A few things that sometimes hold people up:

1. **Not sure if it's worth the setup time** - Fair. Most people see value within the first hour once their agent starts remembering context across sessions.

2. **Technical blockers** - Maybe you're on Windows (we're Mac/Linux for now), or Claude Code isn't set up yet. If that's the case, lmk - I can point you to resources or we can troubleshoot.

3. **Just browsing for now** - Also fine! The Discord has plenty of useful content even without installing.

Is one of these the situation, or something else entirely?

No pressure either way - Signet is free and open source, so you can always come back to it later. Just want to make sure you're not stuck on something I can help with.

-Jake

P.S. If you're curious but want someone to handle the setup, we do offer Done-With-You sessions ($499) where I walk you through everything live. But honestly, most people get it running on their own in under 30 minutes.
Still figuring out the Signet setup? Our #getting-started channel has a 10-min quickstart that covers 90% of questions. Want me to send the direct link? -Jake

7A.subject: Last check-in on Signet
7A.content:
Hey!

This is my last proactive check-in - don't want to be annoying.

Quick summary of where things stand:

- **Signet is free forever** - open source, no catch
- **Discord community** - ask questions anytime in #help
- **Getting started** - takes about 15-30 minutes for most people

If you ever want hands-on help:
- **Done-With-You ($499)** - I walk you through setup live, answer all your questions
- **Done-For-You ($1499)** - I set up everything including a Mac mini with agents pre-configured

But again, most people don't need that. The free path works great.

I'll leave you to it - just reply anytime if something comes up. The Discord #help channel is also fantastic for quick answers.

-Jake

7B.content: Your first memory just hit Signet! That's the hardest part done. Now your agent will start building context. What are you working on today? -Jake

7B.subject: Your Signet is live - here's what happens next
7B.content:
Hey!

Saw your first memory come through - congrats on getting set up!

Here's what to expect over the next few days:

**Day 1-2:** Memories feel random. Your agent is just capturing what it can. This is normal.

**Day 3-7:** Patterns emerge. You'll notice your agent referencing past conversations more naturally.

**Week 2+:** The magic. Your agent genuinely knows you - your projects, preferences, how you think.

Things that accelerate this:
- Use Claude Code for your real work (not just testing)
- Let conversations flow naturally
- Don't worry about "feeding" it perfect info

Things to avoid:
- Resetting your context constantly
- Overthinking what gets saved

The system is designed to be invisible. Just work normally and let Signet do its thing.

Questions? Reply here or drop them in Discord #help.

-Jake
Been about a day since your first Signet memory. How's it feeling so far? Any questions about what it's capturing or how to get more value from it? -Jake

7C.content: Noticed your Signet usage is pretty light so far. Totally normal early on - but curious if there's something specific you're trying to figure out? -Jake

7C.subject: Getting more value from Signet
7C.content:
Hey!

I see you're using Signet but at a pretty light level (a few memories per day). That's totally fine - but I wanted to share what tends to unlock more value.

**Common patterns I see with light usage:**

1. **Only using it for certain tasks** - Signet works best when it's running across ALL your Claude Code work. The connections between unrelated projects are often the most valuable.

2. **Short sessions** - Quick questions don't build much context. Longer working sessions where you're solving real problems generate richer memories.

3. **Haven't explored skills** - Things like `/recall` let you actively query past context. Many people don't know these exist.

4. **Still in "testing" mode** - When you stop testing Signet and just USE it for real work, the value compounds fast.

Any of these resonate?

The goal isn't necessarily more memories - it's memories that capture things worth remembering. Quality over quantity.

Happy to dig into your specific workflow if helpful.

-Jake
Quick tip: Run Signet while doing your normal coding work for a full day. Don't optimize for it - just let it watch. The context builds fast. -Jake

7C.subject: One thing that might help
7C.content:
Hey!

One more thought on getting more from Signet:

The people who get the most value tend to have it running as their default setup. Not something they turn on for specific tasks - just always there.

If your current setup makes that friction-y (maybe Signet isn't auto-starting, or something is awkward), that's worth fixing.

Two paths:

1. **DIY** - Check the Discord #tips-and-tricks channel for optimization guides

2. **Done-With-You ($499)** - 90-minute session where I look at your specific setup and optimize it with you. Most people who do this see 3-5x more value afterward because we eliminate the friction points.

Either way works. Just want you getting real value from this thing.

-Jake

7D.content: Your Signet usage has been solid - 10+ memories daily. You're in the top 20% of users. Curious what you're building that needs all that context? -Jake

7D.subject: You're using Signet more than most
7D.content:
Hey!

Just noticed your usage patterns - you're generating serious context with Signet. That's great to see.

At this level of usage, a few things worth knowing:

1. **Memory quality matters more now** - With more memories, the retrieval system has more to work with. If you're getting less relevant recalls, we can tune some settings.

2. **Skills become more powerful** - Things like `mcp__signet__knowledge_expand` and session summaries get really useful with more data.

3. **You might have insights** - What's working well? What's annoying? Feedback from active users shapes the roadmap.

Also - if you're using Signet this much, you might benefit from a more tuned setup. Our Done-With-You sessions ($499) include optimization for power users. Not necessary, but some people at your usage level find it valuable.

Mostly just wanted to say: glad it's useful.

-Jake
Would you be open to sharing how you're using Signet in the Discord? Always looking for real user stories. No pressure - just think others could learn from it. -Jake

7F.content: Hey - noticed some errors in your Signet logs. Everything okay? Sometimes these are easy fixes, sometimes they're annoying. What's going on? -Jake

7F.subject: Saw some issues with your Signet setup
7F.content:
Hey!

I noticed some error patterns in your Signet usage. Wanted to reach out before it becomes frustrating.

Common issues I see:

1. **Memory storage failures** - Usually a daemon issue. Quick restart typically fixes it.

2. **Retrieval quality problems** - Embeddings might need regeneration or settings need tuning.

3. **Integration conflicts** - Something else in your Claude Code setup conflicting with Signet.

4. **Resource constraints** - Signet can be hungry on older machines.

What are you experiencing specifically?

The reason I'm reaching out proactively: most people who hit issues and don't resolve them quickly end up abandoning Signet. That's a shame because the fixes are usually straightforward.

I'd rather help you troubleshoot now than have you drift away frustrated.

What's happening on your end?

-Jake
If you're stuck on Signet issues - two options: Discord #help channel (free, community answers fast) or reply here and I'll look at it directly. Your call. -Jake

7F.subject: Don't let this issue kill your momentum
7F.content:
Hey!

Following up on the Signet issues.

Here's what I've seen happen: someone hits a technical snag, means to fix it "later," and then never comes back. The initial momentum is lost, and they miss out on what Signet could have done for their workflow.

I don't want that to happen to you.

The issue you're hitting is almost certainly fixable. Most Signet problems fall into a few categories and have known solutions.

Three paths forward:

1. **Discord #help** - Free, community is responsive, good for common issues

2. **Reply here with details** - I'll personally look at it and point you to the fix

3. **Done-With-You ($499)** - If you want me to actually get on a call, look at your setup, and fix it live. This also includes full optimization, so you'd come out of it with everything working perfectly.

What sounds right for your situation?

-Jake
Honest question: Is fixing Signet something you want to prioritize, or is it more of a "nice to have"? No judgment - just want to help appropriately based on that. -Jake

7F.subject: Two ways this usually goes
7F.content:
Hey!

Last note on the Signet issues.

I've seen two patterns:

**Pattern A:** Someone hits an issue, gets busy, means to fix it, never does. Six months later they see Signet mentioned somewhere and think "oh yeah, I should try that again." The cycle repeats.

**Pattern B:** Someone hits an issue, spends 15 minutes getting help, fixes it, and moves on with a working system.

Pattern B is objectively better. The question is just what help looks like for you:

- **Free path:** Discord #help, docs, or reply here with specifics
- **Paid path:** Done-With-You ($499) where I fix it live and optimize everything while I'm there

I'm not going to keep following up after this - don't want to be annoying. But the offer stands if you want help.

-Jake
Last check-in on those Signet issues. If you ever want to revisit, Discord #help is always there. Or reply here anytime - I'll see it. Good luck with everything. -Jake

7G.content:

Your Signet usage went quiet about a week ago. Just wanted to check in.

A few possibilities:

1. **Got busy** - Totally fair. Signet will be here when you're ready.

2. **Something broke** - If there's a technical issue blocking you, let me know. Usually easy to fix.

3. **Didn't see the value** - Also fair. Not every tool is for everyone. If you want to share feedback on what didn't click, I'm always listening.

4. **Switched to something else** - Curious what, if so. We're always learning from alternatives.

No pressure to respond - just wanted you to know the door is open if you want to pick it back up.

-Jake

P.S. Discord community is still active if you have questions.
